1.scene: 自然保育志愿者小组咨询

mcq.q: What regular activity does the group do on the beach?
opts: Clear litter a few hours a month

mcq.q: What is the group currently working on at the nature reserve?
What is the cost of the woodwork session including lunch?

1.errorText: Jan phones Peter to ask about the Buckworth Conservation Group. Peter explains the group spend time on the beach clearing litter. Dogs are not allowed on the beach but can walk on the cliffs. At the nature reserve they look after paths and create new habitats. They have just finished making nesting boxes for birds, and next they plan to work on encouraging insects. They are also running a project to count the different species of moths visiting the reserve. They are replacing a damaged wall on the southern side between the car park and the woodshed. The guided walk to Ruston Island will meet at Dunsmore Beach at low tide. Bring a picnic and waterproof shoes. The woodwork session on the 17th runs 10am–3pm; beginners can make spoons. It costs £35 including lunch.

pragmatic.quote: I was up most of one night helping a sheep deliver a lamb. It wasn't a straightforward birth, but I managed. It was a great feeling to see the lamb stagger to its feet and start feeding.

4.scene: 迷宫与labyrinth历史与现代应用讲座

signpost.quote: Finding your way through a labyrinth involves many twists and turns, but it's not possible to get lost as there is only one single path.
opts: Clarifying a key difference between labyrinths and mazes before explaining labyrinth origins

signpost.quote: Mazes became fashionable in the 15th and 16th centuries in Europe and can still be found in the gardens of great houses.
opts: Introducing the historical context of mazes as a contrast to labyrinths

signpost.quote: The winding spiral of the labyrinth has been used for centuries as a metaphor for life's journey — it helped give people a sense of direction.
opts: Explaining the spiritual and psychological function of labyrinths before providing historical evidence

signpost.quote: More recently labyrinths have experienced something of a revival — some believe that walking one promotes healing and mindfulness.
opts: Introducing a modern trend after covering historical usage

signpost.quote: A pamphlet at Colorado Children's Hospital informs patients that 'walking a labyrinth can often calm people in the midst of a crisis'.
opts: Providing a specific contemporary case study of therapeutic application

in the middle

and restored sense of balance

1.errorText: Frank Pritchard has retired and moved to Southoe and wants to volunteer. Jane co-ordinates the voluntary work. For the library, Frank can help collect books if he has a car, and help keep the records up to date. The library uses the South Room in the village hall. The lunch club for elderly people needs drivers, and Frank can help. Jane needs help with art groups at the club. Frank can take Mrs Carroll to her hospital appointment on Tuesday morning. He will also do some painting in Mr Selsbury's garden. The October 19th quiz needs someone to provide refreshments. For the November dance with 1920s music, Frank can check tickets. The New Year's Eve party will be at the Mountfort Hotel and they need someone to design a poster.

1.scene: 爱尔兰冲浪假期建议咨询

mcq.q: Why does Jack recommend September for surfing in Ireland?
opts: It is the cheapest time to travel
The waves are at their most challenging

mcq.q: What is the daily equipment hire cost for a wetsuit and board?
What additional equipment does Jack strongly recommend?

1.errorText: A woman asks Jack for advice about a surfing holiday in Ireland. He recommends it for all ages and abilities — it is great for families. Surfing helps if you are fit. He recommends County Donegal, and also County Clare — particularly Lahinch, where the waves are not too challenging and the town is lively. There are good hotels and hostels. He has also been to County Mayo, where there is a good surf school at Carrowniskey beach. The summer camp runs for three hours every day for a week. While the children surfed, Jack and his wife rented kayaks to explore the river. September is Jack's favourite month because the weather is best then. The daily hire of a wetsuit and board is about 30 euros but you save 40 euros if you hire by the week. Good quality wetsuits and boots are essential.

4.scene: 鸟类迁徙历史理论讲座

signpost.quote: Until relatively recently, where birds went in the winter was considered something of a mystery — the lack of modern science led to many theories we now see as error-filled.
opts: Establishing the historical ignorance about migration before presenting the main theories

signpost.quote: Aristotle developed the theory of transmutation — the seasonal change of one species into another — by observing redstarts and robins.
opts: Introducing the most influential ancient theory before showing why it was flawed

signpost.quote: The most bizarre theory was put forward by an English amateur scientist, Charles Morton, in the seventeenth century — that birds migrate to the moon and back.
opts: Introducing the most extreme theory as the final example of pre-scientific thinking

signpost.quote: One of the key moments in migration theory came in 1822 when a white stork was shot in Germany with a long spear in its neck from Central Africa.
opts: Introducing the first concrete physical evidence of long-distance bird migration

signpost.quote: This idea came about because it seemed impossible that small birds weighing only a few grams could fly over vast oceans.
opts: Explaining the reasoning behind the large-bird taxi theory before introducing scientific progress

1.scene: 公寓清洁服务套餐咨询

mcq.q: What regular additional service does the client specifically request?
What monthly service does the client want?
What is the length of cleaner training before they are taken on?

1.errorText: A client calls Easy Life Cleaning Services. The basic package for a one-bedroom flat takes about two hours and covers all surfaces, kitchen, bathroom and floors. Additional services include oven cleaning, fridge cleaning, ironing shirts and microwave cleaning. The client asks for fridge cleaning every week and ironing his shirts. Monthly additional services include inside window cleaning and balcony washing; the client wants both. The company can also provide contact details for a reliable plumber and an electrician. They also offer a special vacuum system for people with asthma. All cleaners undergo a police security check and provide references. They undergo three weeks of training, then take a test, and their performance is reviewed by clients after every visit.

errors.correct: ironing shirts for work ✓ — replace: 'asthma' → 'allergies' done

2.scene: 酒店员工留用管理策略演讲

kwt.original: A lot of managers think it's the low pay and long hours that are the main causes of high staff turnover — but what they don't realise is that it's the lack of training.
kwt.transStart: Many managers attribute high staff turnover to low pay and long hours, while
kwt.transEnd: the crucial role of inadequate training.
kwt.collocation: overlooking (= failing to notice or consider something important)

kwt.original: When staff leave, it can also severely affect the colleagues they leave behind — it has a negative effect on remaining staff.
kwt.transStart: When employees depart, their absence has a serious negative
kwt.transEnd: on the morale and performance of the colleagues who remain.
kwt.collocation: impact on (= a strong or marked effect on something or someone)

kwt.original: Make sure you never give certain staff preferential treatment — all staff should be given some choice about when they work.
kwt.transEnd: giving preferential treatment when allocating shifts, ensuring all staff are treated equally.
kwt.collocation: avoid giving (= deliberately refrain from doing something)

options: A.  invited junior staff to meetings to make them feel valued
B.  management programme for junior reception staff
C.  funded childcare vouchers for staff with family responsibilities
D.  management talent scheme to encourage skilled staff to stay
E.  all-expenses trip abroad for 50 high achievers annually

3.scene: 运动科学专业讨论与器材进化
3.functions: A  Describing a pivotal personal experience    B  Expressing a mistaken expectation    C  Identifying an ironic development    D  Describing an accidental innovation    E  Explaining a design reason

pragmatic.quote: I was chosen to go to Kenya out of loads of kids and run with some of the top teenage athletes in the world. But once we started competing, I very quickly realised I wasn't good enough.
pragmatic.speaker: Thomas

pragmatic.quote: In about the 1920s, a factory was making rolls of rubber in bulk for something like horse harnesses — and someone realised it'd make a perfect covering for wooden bats.
pragmatic.speaker: Thomas

pragmatic.quote: When the first cricket helmet came in, in 1978, the Australian batsman who first wore it was booed and jeered by people watching because it was so ugly!
pragmatic.speaker: Thomas

pragmatic.quote: Serious cyclists ended up with wet hair from all the hard exercise — that's why they have large air vents in them now so that the skin can breathe more easily.
pragmatic.speaker: Jeanne

pragmatic.quote: I remember my great grandfather telling me that because a club was made entirely of wood, it would easily break and players had to get another.
pragmatic.speaker: Jeanne

4.scene: 枫糖浆生产历史与工艺讲座

signpost.quote: Maple syrup looks rather like clear honey, but it's not made by bees — it's produced from the plant fluid inside the maple tree.
opts: Introducing the product by correcting a potential misconception before describing its properties

signpost.quote: Canada produces over two-thirds of the world's maple syrup, which is why the five-pointed maple leaf has featured on the flag since 1964.
opts: Connecting production dominance to national identity as context before describing historical origins

signpost.quote: Long before Europeans settled in these parts of the world, the indigenous communities had started producing maple sugar.
opts: Establishing the indigenous origins of maple syrup production before describing modern practices

signpost.quote: What happens is that during a cold night the tree absorbs water from the soil, and then in the warmer daytime the change in temperature causes the water to be pushed back down.
opts: Explaining the biological mechanism behind sap formation to justify why climate is so important

signpost.quote: It takes 40 litres of sap to produce one litre of maple syrup — so you can get an idea of how much is needed!
opts: Providing a striking statistic to emphasise the scale of effort required
